title: caprifoliales
intro: The rank caprifoliales is an order of flowering plants, characterized by having five sepals and five petals in a symmetrical arrangement. This order includes a variety of plant families, such as the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae), which comprises plants like Lonicera and Diervilla. Other notable families within the caprifoliales order include the cinquefoil family (Adoxaceae) and the heath family (Adoxaceae) is not a member but the honeysuckle family is. Additionally, some sources group certain plant families, such as the willow family (Salicaceae) and the hawthorn family (Crataegaceae), under the caprifoliales order.

families.family_desc: Caprifoliaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the honeysuckle or elderberry family, which includes over 800 species of shrubs and vines.
